SEARCH
Package net.sourceforge.plantuml.openpdf

Class PdfGraphics

java.lang.Object
net.sourceforge.plantuml.openpdf.PdfGraphics
public class PdfGraphics extends Object
PDF backend that mirrors the public API of net.sourceforge.plantuml.klimt.drawing.svg.SvgGraphics.

Coordinate convention

PDF natively has Y growing upwards from a bottom-left origin; SVG and PlantUML have Y growing downwards from a top-left origin. Rather than flipping the CTM (which forces every text run and image to compensate with a local flip), this driver keeps PDF's native Y-up convention and simply negates Y in input: a caller-supplied SVG-coord (x, y) is drawn at PDF-coord (x, -y).

For shapes that take a top-left corner plus a size (rectangles, raster images), the bottom-left in PDF coords becomes (x, -(y + height)). For point-like primitives (line endpoints, polygon vertices, ellipse centers, text baselines, path segments) it is simply (x, -y).

From negative-Y back to a normal page

The content is buffered into a PdfTemplate (a PDF form XObject) while we draw. The template is unbounded - it can hold negative-Y content without complaining. At createPdf(OutputStream) time we know the final extent (maxX, maxY in SVG coords): we open a page of size maxX x maxY and place the template translated by (0, maxY) so the content lands inside [0, 0, maxX, maxY].

Net result for callers: SVG-style coords on the way in, a standard-shape page on the way out, no CTM flip anywhere, no per-call compensation for text or images.

Helper ny(double)

All internal conversions go through ny(double) (read it as "negate y"). If anything ever looks upside-down or misplaced in a generated PDF, the one place to look is whether a coordinate took the ny() route or leaked through as a positive PDF coord.

Deliberately omitted in v1

Drop shadows, embedded SVG images (stdlib icons), gradients, text-background filters, rotated text, CSS classes, interactive scripts, dark mode.

Not for TeaVM

OpenPDF depends on java.awt and java.util.zip; this driver is server-side only and is excluded from the TeaVM build path.

OpenPDF version

Targets OpenPDF 3.0+ which uses the org.openpdf package namespace. The legacy com.lowagie packages have been removed in 3.0 and are not available anymore.

  • Method Details

    • ensureVisible

      protected final void ensureVisible(double x, double y)
      Tracked in SVG coords (positive Y). maxX and maxY are the largest values the caller has drawn at; the final page will be a maxX x maxY rectangle in standard PDF coords.

    • quadto

      public void quadto(double x0, double y0, double x1, double y1, double x2, double y2)
      Quadratic bezier. PDF does not have a native quad operator, so we convert to a cubic on the fly using the standard formula C1 = P0 + 2/3*(P1-P0), C2 = P2 + 2/3*(P1-P2). The previous current point on the path is needed - we accept it as input because we do not track the path cursor ourselves. Note: the conversion is done in SVG coords for clarity, then Y is negated at the very last moment when calling curveTo. Negating before or after the convex combination gives the same result mathematically, but doing it last keeps the formula readable.

    • fill

      public void fill(int windingRule)

    • drawPathIterator

      public void drawPathIterator(double x, double y, PathIterator path)
      Replay a Java2D PathIterator into the current PDF stream. Used by the higher-level driver to handle arbitrary AWT shapes (including arcs already flattened to bezier by Java2D). All coordinates go through the regular path API (moveto(double, double), lineto(double, double), quadto(double, double, double, double, double, double), curveto(double, double, double, double, double, double)), so Y negation happens transparently there.

    • pdfText

      public void pdfText(String text, double x, double y, org.openpdf.text.pdf.BaseFont font, int fontSize)
      Render a single line of text at the (x, y) baseline position. With Y negation, the default PDF text matrix (which renders glyphs right-side-up) is what we want - no per-call flip is needed. We just use PdfContentByte.setTextMatrix(float, float) to place the baseline at (x, -y).

    • pdfImage

      public void pdfImage(byte[] pngOrJpegBytes, double x, double y, double width, double height)
      Insert a raster image (PNG/JPEG bytes) at position (x, y). With Y negation, the image's bottom-left in PDF coords lands at (x, -(y + height)) - same formula as for rectangles. No local matrix flip is needed; the image stays right-side-up.
      Parameters:
      pngOrJpegBytes - encoded image data.
      x - top-left X (SVG convention).
      y - top-left Y (SVG convention).
      width - target render width.
      height - target render height.

    • openLink

      public void openLink(String url, String title)
      Begin a clickable region. Currently the bounding box of the link is driven externally (callers must drive ensureVisible(double, double) - the higher-level UGraphic driver does that anyway). A future iteration may auto-track inside openLink/closeLink by intercepting every drawing primitive between the two calls. Unlike SVG, PDF link annotations are flat rectangles - we cannot nest them. Nested calls do behave correctly because we keep a stack of PdfGraphics.PendingLink, but they will produce sibling rectangles rather than truly nested clickable regions.

    • closeLink

      public void closeLink()

    • createPdf

      public void createPdf(OutputStream os) throws IOException
      Flush the buffered drawing to the supplied stream as a complete PDF. Pipeline:
      1. Compute the final page size from maxX and maxY.
      2. Switch the document's page size to maxX x maxY and start a new page (the placeholder page we opened with is replaced).
      3. Place the PdfTemplate on that page with a translation of (0, maxY): our negative-Y content moves into [0, 0, maxX, maxY] in page coords.
      After this call the instance must not be used anymore.
      Throws:
      IOException

    • getRawContent

      public org.openpdf.text.pdf.PdfContentByte getRawContent()
      Direct access to the underlying OpenPDF content stream. Intended for advanced cases (custom shading patterns, form XObjects, ...). Most callers should never need this. Coordinate warning: raw drawing through this stream uses native PDF coords (Y-up). Either work entirely in PDF coords, or negate Y yourself with the same convention this class uses (pdfY = -svgY).

    • getRawWriter

      public org.openpdf.text.pdf.PdfWriter getRawWriter()

    • pdfPath

      public void pdfPath(double x, double y, UPath path)
      Render a UPath (PlantUML's SVG-flavored path representation) translated by (x, y). Each USegment maps to a native PDF path operator, except USegmentType.SEG_ARCTO which is converted on the fly to a sequence of cubic Beziers (PDF has no arc operator). The conversion follows the SVG 1.1 Appendix F.6 endpoint-to-center parametrization and approximates each ~90 degree slice with one cubic.

      Path metadata (comment, codeLine) is currently ignored: PDF has no clean place to expose it (no element id like SVG). A future iteration could emit it as a marked-content tag if useful for accessibility or tooling.
